items.name: Remove Your Profile form from Log Weight page
items.detail: The long profile form (name/height/age/gender/activity/goal/hyrox/training-days/equipment + Maintenance/Daily-Calorie-Goal display) was extracted into a new renderProfileForm() and MOVED to the Settings tab (new 'Profile' section, right under Account). It was moved, not deleted, because those fields drive the app-wide calorie/macro math. All input IDs/data-attrs are unchanged so the existing profile handlers bind identically on Settings. Verified: editing height in Settings persists to hx_profile and re-renders.

items.name: Premium Log Weight card
items.detail: New card at the top of the Profile (body) tab: Current Weight (large mono), last-updated date, Add Weight button (scrolls to + focuses the existing Log Entry weight field), weight trend graph (reuses sparklineChart with the blue --color-interactive accent), and a 3-stat row Highest / Lowest / 7-Day change. View Weight History button expands the full history and scrolls to it. No body-fat/height/gender/age/goal/activity/equipment/hyrox on this page. Empty state handled (no weigh-ins => 'from your profile' + 'log two weigh-ins to see your trend').

items.name: Calculator section + BMI calculator
items.detail: Card grid below Log Weight: BMI, BMR, TDEE, Daily Calories, Macros + a 'View All Calculators' card, all master-UI premium-card style. Tapping a card (or View All) opens a dedicated in-tab calculator view (state.bodyView='calculators', back button) using the existing renderCalculators() engine. Added a REAL BMI calculator (it was genuinely missing): new CALCULATORS entry, a render block, and a run-calculator branch computing weight/(height/100)^2 with standard category bands. TDEE and Daily Calories both open the existing 'calorie' calc (which computes both maintenance TDEE and the goal-adjusted target); Macros opens the 'protein' calc; View All exposes the full 10-calculator picker (carbs/fat/lbm/ideal/bodyfat/bodytype/hr). No existing formula changed.

items.name: My Routines readability
items.detail: Routine name is now 19px, font-weight 700 (semi-bold), line-height 1.3, flex:1 min-width:0 with overflow-wrap:anywhere so a long name wraps to a second line instead of truncating; card padding increased to 18px, delete button aligned top and shrink-locked. Verified a 44-char name wraps to 2 lines at 360px with no truncation and no page overflow.

items.name: Workout search keyboard bug (root-cause fix)
items.rootCause: The exercise-picker search input's handler called the global render() on every keystroke. render() rebuilds the ENTIRE app via #app.innerHTML = ..., which DESTROYS and recreates the <input> element. Android (and mobile WebViews generally) dismiss the soft keyboard the instant the focused element is removed from the DOM; the old setTimeout(0) that re-focused the freshly-created input then reopened the keyboard -> the observed dismiss-then-reopen flicker on every character. Not a React key/remount issue (the app is vanilla JS with full-innerHTML rendering) but the same class of bug: focus lost because the focused node is replaced.
items.solution: Split the picker so the header + search <input> + filters render ONCE per full render, and only the results list lives in a stable #ex-picker-results container. The input handler now updates ONLY that container's innerHTML (via a small 120ms debounce), never touching the <input>. Because the input element is never destroyed, focus/keyboard/caret are preserved automatically with zero re-focus tricks. Row clicks were switched to a single delegated listener on the stable container so they keep working after each in-place results refresh. Removed the old render()+setTimeout re-focus hack entirely.
items.verified: Tagged the input element with a custom property, focused it, dispatched 5 real input events ('bench'): the tag survived (same element, never recreated), document.activeElement stayed the input the whole time, value became 'bench', results filtered to 11 matches, and picking a filtered result still added the exercise. Zero console errors.

items.name: Search performance
items.detail: Input value updates instantly (native), only the results list rebuilds and only after a 120ms debounce, so a fast typist doesn't rebuild the ~478-row list on every keystroke. No full-app re-render per keystroke anymore. Added autocomplete/autocorrect/autocapitalize/spellcheck=off on the search input for cleaner mobile typing.

items.name: UI consistency
items.detail: Log Weight + Calculator sections use .premium-card, .section-heading, --color-interactive (blue) accents, --color-border, --color-text-secondary, radius/shadow tokens, and the shared button classes -- identical to the master design system, responsive, and theme-aware (light + dark).

items.name: Preserve existing functionality
items.detail: Body Composition, Log Entry (weight+sleep+hrv+measurements), Body History, Body Progress Photos, Records & Achievements all remain on the body tab. Calculator formulas unchanged (only BMI added). No storage keys renamed/removed (bodyView is transient, not persisted). Health Connect / nutrition / AI Coach / progress untouched. Regression spot-checks all clean.

knownLimitations: The actual on-device soft-keyboard behavior was proven by the DOM-level root-cause fix (input element persists + focus retained), not by observing a physical Android keyboard -- that remains a real-device confirmation.

TDEE and Daily Calories cards both open the same underlying 'calorie' calculator (which genuinely outputs both maintenance and the goal-adjusted target); this is intentional given the existing calculator set.
Screenshot capture unavailable in this environment; verification is DOM/computed-style/behavioral, not rendered images.
